settings.text:
Sparkhaus opened in Denver in 2017 with one van, two cleaners, and a spreadsheet. Eight years later we're a team of nine — still small, still local, still intentionally choosing to not turn into a franchise.

We pay our cleaners as full-time W-2 employees with benefits, because cleaning is skilled work and good people don't stay at places that treat it otherwise. We keep the service area tight — Denver proper and the immediate metro — because a 45-minute drive to a cleaning job is 45 minutes the cleaner isn't paid to stand in.

It's boring-but-deliberate operations. You feel the results in the work.
Licensing, insurance, and the stuff that matters when something goes wrong.
Most cleaning-service websites bury this page. We put it here because it's the first thing a reasonable person should ask before handing over a key.

settings.features:
+ Colorado DORA licensed, bonded since 2017
+ $2,000,000 commercial general liability coverage
+ Workers' compensation on every employee (not contractors)
+ Green Seal-certified eco-friendly products by default
+ 24-hour satisfaction guarantee on every clean

settings.name: Amara Osei
settings.bio: Started Sparkhaus after running cleaning operations for a boutique hotel. Runs scheduling, hiring, and the client-relationship side of the business. Still personally interviews every cleaner before they join the team.

settings.name: Daniela Reyes
settings.bio: Leads deep-clean and move-in/out bookings. Seven-year veteran. The one you want for the jobs nobody else wants to take on. Fluent in Spanish; speaks more English than she lets on.

settings.name: Chris Pham
settings.bio: Anchors 40 biweekly and weekly clients across Capitol Hill and Congress Park. Clients request him back by name. Previously ran hospital housekeeping — knows infection-control cleaning cold.

settings.name: Tess Kowalski
settings.bio: Runs post-construction crews. Started in commercial janitorial, moved to high-end residential renovations. Calm under pressure, thorough on the details, respectful of finishes.

settings.label: Denver neighborhoods
settings.description: Where we service weekly or more

settings.title: Eight years, at a measured pace.
Founded
settings.description: Amara launches Sparkhaus from a one-car garage in Park Hill with one van and two cleaners. First hundred clients all come by word of mouth through the hotel she used to run.

settings.title: Through the pandemic, by way of commercial
settings.description: When residential cleaning pauses in March, the team pivots to commercial sanitising for essential-business clients — clinics, food pantries, shelters. Residential resumes in the summer; commercial stays as a secondary practice.

settings.title: Past 3,000 homes cleaned
settings.description: Team grows to six. Introduce the 24-hour satisfaction guarantee as a formal policy — had always been how we handled complaints informally; now it's on every booking confirmation.

settings.title: Nine cleaners, 5,000+ homes, fourteen neighborhoods
settings.description: Today. Full-time W-2 team with benefits. Locally owned, still intentionally small. Amara still interviews every hire. We still say no to clients outside the service area because the math for them doesn't work.
